name: Player Profile Stats
description: Long-term account or profile statistics that summarize play history, milestones, performance, preferences, and completion across sessions.
design_notes: Profile stats are useful when they are trustworthy and explainable. The design should define which events increment counters, which values are private, how cross-save merges resolve conflicts, how platform services sync records, and whether stats can drive achievements, matchmaking, or personalization.
design_purpose: Give players a durable record of activity, mastery, and preferences.
Support achievements, collection logs, matchmaking, recommendations, or social profile pages.
Provide designers and support tools with account-level state that is not tied to one save file.
player_fantasy: The player feels their profile reflects a history of choices, skill, discoveries, and personal milestones.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Account identity that owns profile records and sync state.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.typical_range: profile slot or character profile id
parameters.description: Profile segment tracked independently under the account.

parameters.name: profile_stat_id
parameters.description: Stable stat key used by UI, achievements, and migration code.

parameters.name: stat_increment_event_id
parameters.description: Committed gameplay event that changes a profile stat.

parameters.name: profile_snapshot_id
parameters.description: Saved profile revision used for sync and support comparisons.

parameters.name: cross_save_profile_id
parameters.description: Identifier used to merge profile data across platforms or devices.

parameters.name: privacy_visibility_id
parameters.description: Visibility rule for profile records shown outside local UI.

edge_cases: Two devices update the same profile stat offline and later sync with different snapshots.
A stat is renamed or split during a save migration.
An achievement checks a stat before the profile transaction commits.
Privacy settings change after profile data was already cached by a social page.
A support tool edits account data and must not break anti-cheat validation.
common_bugs: Stats increment from predicted events that later fail authoritative validation.
Cross-save merge keeps the newest snapshot and discards higher personal best records.
Profile exports include private stats despite privacy_visibility_id restrictions.
Counters are stored as localized display strings rather than numeric or structured state.
Platform API sync retries increment local stats again instead of resubmitting the same record.
balancing_notes: Stats used for matchmaking or recommendations should be resistant to farming and clearly scoped.
Public stats can motivate mastery, but overexposure can pressure players away from experimentation.
Lifetime counters are often less meaningful than records tied to mode, difficulty, or rule set.
accessibility_notes: Let players hide or simplify public profile stats that create social pressure.
Provide searchable, sortable, and text-readable stats for large profiles.
Avoid presenting progress exclusively through small badges or color-coded medals.

implementation_notes.unity: Route profile stat changes through an account-profile service that accepts stat_increment_event_id records after gameplay commits. Store profile_snapshot_id and privacy_visibility_id with the data used for platform sync or social display.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep profile stats in a versioned profile resource separate from moment-to-moment save data. Apply migrations by stable profile_stat_id and merge cross-save snapshots with per-stat policies such as max, sum, latest, or authoritative.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use SaveGame or backend profile records for local stats and Online Subsystem calls for platform service sync. Server-authoritative modes should submit validated stat events rather than accepting client-side counters directly.
implementation_notes.web: Store stats in account storage or IndexedDB with event ids and profile revisions. When offline play is supported, queue committed stat events and reconcile them against server state without replaying duplicate increments.

relationships.reason: Profile stats provide counters that achievements can evaluate retroactively or display publicly.
Daily challenge history and claim state can be summarized through profile stats.
Profile stats shown in lobbies need privacy rules and stale-data handling.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses player banners and tracked profile statistics.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Tracks long-term character and account achievements and statistics.
